mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive short-term momentum and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during industrial production expansions in > 70% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation alongside elevated policy rates creates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of periods where both inflation and rates exceed the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for payroll growth falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or forward returns are negative during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand deterioration, prompting equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following housing start declines exceeding the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with accuracy exceeding 52% in out-of-sample testing over a 2-year horizon.
